# Cerno

> Cerno is an open-source human-verification SDK for sensitive actions. The visible task is a maze. The decision comes from a server-side pipeline: proof-of-work, path validation, behavioral scoring, optional Stroop probes, optional WebAuthn, and replay-resistant verification tokens. No ML inference, no third-party calls.

- Packages: `@cernosh/core`, `@cernosh/react`, `@cernosh/server`
- Site: https://cerno.sh
- GitHub: https://github.com/PlawIO/cerno
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/org/cernosh

## Overview

- [Introduction](https://cerno.sh/docs/getting-started/): What Cerno ships, what it verifies, and how the stack fits together
- [Quick Start](https://cerno.sh/docs/quick-start/): Minimum working integration with React and server packages

## Guides

- [React Component](https://cerno.sh/docs/guides/react/): Widget surface, lifecycle, props, and extension points
- [Server SDK](https://cerno.sh/docs/guides/server/): Challenge issuance, verification pipeline, probe timing, WebAuthn, and storage contracts
- [Cloudflare Workers](https://cerno.sh/docs/guides/cloudflare-workers/): Deploy the verification API to CF Workers with Durable Objects
- [Stroop Probes](https://cerno.sh/docs/guides/stroop-probes/): Server-timed cognitive probes that arm mid-solve and complete with signed tokens
- [WebAuthn Attestation](https://cerno.sh/docs/guides/webauthn/): Full registration and assertion verification with stable identity binding
- [Adaptive Proof-of-Work](https://cerno.sh/docs/guides/adaptive-pow/): How PoW difficulty adjusts based on client signals and trust scores
- [Reputation System](https://cerno.sh/docs/guides/reputation/): Cross-session behavioral trust that accumulates over time

## Concepts

- [Behavioral Scoring](https://cerno.sh/docs/concepts/behavioral-scoring/): How Cerno scores human vs. AI behavior using maze-relative baselines
- [Secret Features](https://cerno.sh/docs/concepts/secret-features/): Server-only behavioral metrics for defense-in-depth
- [Threat Model](https://cerno.sh/docs/concepts/threat-model/): Who Cerno is designed to stop and what the attacker looks like

## Reference

- [Challenge API](https://cerno.sh/docs/reference/api/): Request and response payloads for challenge, probe, verification, and WebAuthn flows
- [siteverify](https://cerno.sh/docs/reference/siteverify/): Server-to-server token verification endpoint
- [Error Codes](https://cerno.sh/docs/reference/errors/): All error codes returned by the verification API
